proof_limits: compose_building assembles caller / COO declared node and edge choices only

constraints: Read-only; no file edits, no git mutation, no hook/SDK execution (Brick declares no write_scope; native_grant write_effective=false).
Do not run broad --all (task source constraint); classification is structural inference from file bodies, not measured runtime.

Registry-closure coupling: check_profile.py self-test asserts every profile kernel_checks id is in KERNEL_DISPATCH, every rule key in RULE_RUNNERS, and core.yaml path_allowlist enumerates EVERY profile file. Adding/moving any profile file forces a shared edit to support/checkers/profiles/core.yaml — so profile-diet Buildings cannot have fully parallel write scopes over profiles/** + core.yaml.

module_registry.yaml is read by the G4 elegance/path-admission guard against the live file tree: every module add/delete/rename forces a registry edit, so module-cleanup Buildings serialize on that one file.
--all globs every profiles/*.yaml, so a live-provider profile cannot live under profiles/ without being pulled into the deterministic suite.

Tree divergence: architecture-map.md, module_registry.yaml, core.yaml, and the 24 profiles were all read from the cwd worktree /Users/smith/.brick/worktrees/struct-surgery-0623; the prior building noted /Users/smith/projects/BRICK diverges (preset count 27 vs 28). Diet/cleanup Buildings must pin to one canonical checkout before counts/ids are trusted.

The 0611 split map (status/kernel/checker-split-map-0611.md) is a dated SUPPORT record, not current truth: it names profiles (post_d_surface_recompile_p7, a p7 profile) absent from the current 24-profile set, so its dispositions must be re-verified before execution.
missing_scope: The task source declares no target end-state profile count or module count for the diet, so 'done' has no numeric boundary — only structural stop conditions.
Per-profile and per-module runtime/cost weight is unmeasured (no execution); 'heavy/duplicative' is inferred from file size, folded-case count, and registry family size.

Precise per-profile family assignment was not exhaustively verified: only core.yaml (full), module_registry.yaml (full), and 8 profile headers were read; the remaining profile bodies were not line-read, so the six-family map below is a candidate to be confirmed by the follow-on Building.
Which profile hosts reporter_notification_projection is not pinned (grep shows the string across 5 profiles); registry pins it to reporter.py/report_sinks.py but the host profile must be confirmed.
No reproduction of whether any profile makes a live network call (inferred deterministic from docstrings/mocks, not executed).
not_proven: Precise per-profile family membership — the six-family map is a candidate grounded in core.yaml + module_registry.yaml + 8 profile headers; the remaining profile bodies were not line-read.
Which profile hosts the reporter_notification_projection kernel check (string appears across 5 profiles; not pinned by read).
Profile/module execution cost or timing — no profile or --all was run (constraint); 'heavy/oversized' is structural inference, not measured runtime.
That no profile makes a live network/provider call — inferred from docstrings/mocks, not from execution.
Canonical-tree counts: the worktree shows 24 profiles and 28 presets; the /Users/smith/projects/BRICK tree was reported to differ (27 presets), so exact ids/counts must be re-pinned on one checkout.
Current validity of the 0611 split-map dispositions (it references profiles absent from the current set).
Whether the proposed Building scopes are truly disjoint beyond the named chokepoints; only the core.yaml / module_registry.yaml / checker-lib shared files were identified.
Source truth, success, quality, and Movement are not judged here; the DECLARED skill manifest does not prove which skills were actually fetched.
Whether the prior building's HOLD reroute fired — only the recorded non-binding concern is observed, not the Link disposition.
returned.objective_summary: Read-only plan boundary for the P9 checker/module diet follow-up. The prior design Building (p9-checker-profile-diet-design-0625) HOLD'd with a non-binding design_gap concern: it returned a flat 24-profile list and deferred the six-family taxonomy plus the customer-proof and reporter/projection families to a future doc Building. This follow-up bounds the work to (a) deliver the explicit six-family checker/profile map, (b) add a module-cleanup angle grounded in support/docs/references/architecture-map.md + support/checkers/module_registry.yaml (god modules, duplicate support families, stale/dead surfaces, module-boundary risk), (c) separate delete/merge-now vs needs-admission/checker-first, (d) surface write-scope conflicts (core.yaml, module_registry.yaml, case_runners.py), and (e) propose 3-6 sequenceable follow-on Buildings. No files are edited; output is support evidence only and judges no success/quality/Movement.
observed_evidence: Prior HOLD reproduced: p9-checker-profile-diet-design-0625 closure transition-concern (binding:false, concern_kind design_gap, concern_ref ...six-family-classification-partial) points related_boundary_refs at brick:p9-checker-profile-diet-design-0625-plan; reason: the plan returned a flat 24-profile list and deferred the family taxonomy + customer-proof + reporter/projection families to proposed Building B4.

CANDIDATE six-family map (worktree 24 profiles + core.yaml kernel checks; profiles can carry checks of MORE than one family, which is itself a diet finding): [admission] package_path_admission, project_declaration, agent_resource_resolution, declared_verifier_exists, building_root_anchor, catalog_reader_sync (core), charter_injection, driver_public_intake_seal, brick_cli_entrypoint; [boundary] axis_contract_projection, axis_field_enum_parity, axis_field_set_single_source, support_no_axis_judgment, link_gate_measurement_separation, axis_crossing_elegance, builder_consumes_axis_api, agentfact_single_home (core), gate_registry_single_source, agent_object_schema_single_source, return_field_merge_set_parity, assembly_equivalence; [runtime smoke, deterministic/mocked] cli_runner_stdin_devnull, report_env_autoload, mcp_dispatch_wire, agent_axis_behavioral, adapter_usage_meter; [deterministic fixture] building_automation, structure_template_integrity, native_dispatch_brick_backstop, bounded_agent_proposed_routing_loop, link_routing_behavioral, chained_carry_dependency + core fixture checks (carry_completeness, bricks_spec_completeness, building_map_graph, building_lifecycle_path_shape, fan_out_sibling_evidence_independence, evidence_spine, evidence_spine_projection); [customer proof] tier_a_three_axis_conformance, building_operator_driver0, coo_operating_chain; [reporter/dashboard/product projection] read_side_projection_boundary, coo_operating_chain (closure projection), report_env_autoload (sink env), reporter_notification_projection kernel check (host profile to confirm).

God-module status (module_registry.yaml decomposition_ceilings): the four historical god-modules are ALREADY decomposed to thin facades with owns_crossings=0 and ceiling=0 marked DONE — building_operation.py (bo-god-0, P3d), evidence_assembly.py (ea-god-0, P3b), dynamic_walker.py (dw-god-0, P3c), check_profile.py (check-profile-god-0, P3a). Only report_sinks.py carries a live ceiling (rs-sink-ceiling-0, ceiling=1, deliberate 4-sink seam). So by the registry's own ownership measure no live god-module remains; the cleanup angle is facade/family hygiene, not god-module splitting.

Oversized/duplicate support families (registry rows, candidates for review NOT auto-deletion): composition_*.py = 9 modules (composition_problem/common/kinds/route_policy/gate_translation/graph_emit/graph_validate/compose/intent); walker_*.py = ~9 modules; support/connection/adapter_*.py = 7 pure-relocation siblings behind a still-~1185-line agent_adapter.py facade; support/recording/claims_*.py = 6 modules; support/onboarding hooks = 4 template files. Each was a deliberate decomposition, so 're-merge' must be justified against the G2 (owns<=1) elegance target, not just line count.

Stale/legacy surfaces (architecture-map.md + split map): brick/building_plans/ (~16 legacy hand-declared plans) is 'no longer the entry' (preset single-entry now) but is load-bearing as checker fixtures for strict plan-boundary (split map §1-2) — deletion requires fixture twins first. The 0611 split map also names post_d_surface_recompile_p7 / a p7 profile that are NOT in the current 24-profile set, so those dispositions are partly already executed or stale.

Write-scope conflict surfaces (from constraints): support/checkers/profiles/core.yaml (path_allowlist + closure) is the shared chokepoint for ALL profile add/move/split Buildings; support/checkers/module_registry.yaml is the shared chokepoint for ALL module add/delete/rename Buildings; support/checkers/lib/case_runners.py + kernel_checks.py + rule_runners.py are multi-profile-pinned checker-libs (editing risks many profiles at once). These force sequential ordering whenever two Buildings would touch the same chokepoint.

Phasing observation: delete/merge-NOW (low risk) = phase-name renames (split map E) and exact-duplicate path_exists pins (split map D-1/D-2) IF still present on the canonical tree; needs-admission/checker-FIRST = anything pinned by strict plan-boundary, session-id allowlist, native_dispatch posA, or per-profile building_plan_boundary rows — deleting these before fixture twins yields a silent green (a dead check).

proposed_next_work_boundaries.ordering: Parallel-safe (disjoint scope); should run first as it carries the taxonomy the others cite.
